name: United States v. Neustadt
summary: Noted in Footnote 16 that 'deceit' encompassed all kinds of misrepresentation under the Restatement (First) of Torts, yet Congress included both terms in the FTCA.
significance: Cited by petitioner to show that Congress used overlapping terms ('deceit' and 'misrepresentation') in the FTCA's intentional tort exception, supporting a belt-and-suspenders reading of the postal exception.
